Off Calendar Jul-27-2017 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Elizabeth Branham of HAWKINS PARNELL THACKSTON & YOUNG (415-766-3200), appearing for defendant / moving party John Crane Inc.
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Mary Colman, as successor-in-interest to and as wrongful death heir of Raymond Colman, deceased, et al

RULING:
DEFENDANT JOHN CRANE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION ON PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant John Crane Inc.'s motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' punitive damages claim. Even if the Court considers all of defendant's evidence to which plaintiffs object, it is not sufficient to sustain defendant's burden. Defendant failed to demonstrate that public policy dictates that its motion should be granted. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Reporter: Early K. Langley of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #3537 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/CML)

MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / moving parties Raymond Colman and Mary Colman
Evanthia M. Spanos of BERRY & BERRY (510-250-0200), Designated Defense Counsel
(Refer to Sign-in Sheet for all other appearances.)
RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held and the Court ordered the following: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted per C.C.P. 36(a) and (d). Counsel for plaintiffs demonstrates that Asbestos Corporation Limited and A.W. Chesterton Company have been properly served with the summons and complaint. The trial date is February 8, 2016 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day of trial is March 23, 2016. The Trial Case Management Order dated May 7, 2014 and the Informal Discovery Conference Order dated July 17, 2015 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is Feb. 4, 2016. Plaintiffs stipulate that Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on shorten notice of 15 days, with oppositions due 5 days and replies due 2 days before the hearing. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 15 days, and verifications to 20 days. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than Dec. 4, 2015. Responses to the demand are due no later than Dec. 18, 2015. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by Jan. 29, 2016 at 3:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:00a.m. on Feb. 4, 2016. Fact discovery cut-off date is Jan. 25, 2016. Expert discovery cut-off date is Feb. 5, 2016. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than Dec. 4, 2015. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. No later than Dec. 1, 2015 plaintiffs must provide a statement stating, as to each defendant, the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims. No later than Dec. 22, 2015 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on Dec. 18, 2015 at 11:00am. No need for any party to file any CMC statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. There will be a mandatory settlement conference with the Court pursuant to S.F.L.R. 5 on Feb. 8, 2016 at 1:30p.m. in Department 503. The parties shall comply with Local Rule 5.0 and provide a confidential settlement statement by Feb. 4, 2016 at 2:00p.m. Counsel for plaintiffs to prepare a proposed order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tara L. Williams of AIKEN WELCH, CSR #13428 (Tel: 510-451-1580) (503/TLJ)
